subject: [PATCH v2 016/110] 9p: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the PRIino format being used in debug messages, specifically that it should be used consistently throughout the code. The author agrees to use PRIino consistently and does not mention any plans for further revision.

reviews.author: Christian Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch uses PRIino format for i_ino in various debug messages, but did not explicitly mention any issues or concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Christian Schoenebeck <linux_oss@crudebyte.com>





_______________________________________________
Linux-f2fs-devel mailing list
Linux-f2fs-devel@lists.sourceforge.net
https://lists.sourceforge.net/lists/listinfo/linux-f2fs-devel

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the 9p file system to use the PRIino format macro for printing inode numbers (i_ino). This is a preparatory step for widening i_ino via kino_t, which will allow it to hold larger values. The changes affect several files in the fs/9p directory and involve replacing hardcoded format strings with the PRIino macro.
